archetype: ARCHETYPE-3 (Runtime/Behavior) for `@netscript/plugin-sagas-core` durability seam + ARCHETYPE-5 (Plugin Package) for `@netscript/plugin-sagas` where the concrete `KvSagaStore` and composition-root wiring live. Both packages are stateful saga runtime surfaces; the core defines the port/engine, the plugin owns the durable adapter + composition root, exactly mirroring the proven triggers pattern.
scopeOverlays: SCOPE-service (composition root in plugins/sagas/services/src/main.ts wires runtime resources)
lockedDecisions: Durable store = `KvSagaStore implements SagaStorePort` backed by raw `Deno.Kv` (NOT @saga-bus/store-prisma, NOT Postgres/Prisma write path), mirroring the proven `KvTriggerEventStore` pattern 1:1.

Store + default wiring live in the PLUGIN package `@netscript/plugin-sagas` (`plugins/sagas/src/runtime/`), NOT in core `@netscript/plugin-sagas-core`, to keep core platform-neutral and KV-free — exactly as triggers keeps its KV stores in the plugin.
KV opener `openSagaRuntimeKv()` = `Deno.openKv(Deno.env.get('NETSCRIPT_SAGA_KV_PATH'))`; KV key layout prefix `['sagas']` with `state`/`correlation`/`transition` sub-namespaces.
New plugin factory `createDurableSagaRuntime()` is the durable-by-default composition entrypoint; `createSagaRuntime` core signature is unchanged (store-agnostic).
Service `main.ts:63` and standalone `saga-runner`/`saga-supervisor` default to `createDurableSagaRuntime`; injection seams preserved for tests.
Core `createNativeBus` emits a one-time warn via existing `LoggerPort` when built store-less (kills silent non-durability) instead of forcing a default store into core.
Optimistic `save({expectedVersion})` maps to an atomic Deno.Kv `check(versionstamp)`; stale write rejected with `SagasError.validationFailed`, matching MemorySagaStore.
`sagas.prisma` Postgres tables reclassified as API read-model/projection; dead `@saga-bus/store-prisma` references removed. No npm dep / catalog / version-pin changes.
Read-path reconciliation (v1-handlers Prisma/kvdex branches consuming the new store) is explicitly DEFERRED to a separate slice — deferred-safe.
contracts: SagaStorePort (packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/ports/saga-store-port.ts:23) — REUSED unchanged; KvSagaStore is its first production implementor.
KvSagaStore class + KvSagaStoreOptions ({ kv: Deno.Kv; prefix?: readonly Deno.KvKeyPart[]; now?: () => Date }) — new plugin runtime surface.
openSagaRuntimeKv(): Promise<Deno.Kv> — new plugin runtime helper.
createDurableSagaRuntime(options?): { runtime: SagaRuntime<'native'>; store: SagaStorePort; kv: Deno.Kv } + DurableSagaRuntimeOptions — new plugin composition factory.
create-saga-runtime SagaRuntimeNativeOptions.logger? passthrough (optional) for the store-less warn seam — core.
testPlan: unit: KvSagaStore round-trips SagaStateEnvelope (save→load), correlation save→findByCorrelation, appendTransition log ordering, delete clears state+transitions+correlation (over Deno.openKv(':memory:')).
unit failure-path: KvSagaStore.save with stale expectedVersion rejects with SagasError.validationFailed (parity with MemorySagaStore).
unit: createDurableSagaRuntime injects KvSagaStore by default, honors injected store/kv, and the returned kv is closeable.
unit: core createNativeBus emits exactly one warn via injected LoggerPort when store-less; emits none when a store is present.
integration (cross-restart durability): two sequential runtimes over the SAME in-memory Deno.Kv — second runtime resumes persisted saga state/version instead of initialState for a correlated message.
unit: standalone saga-runner/saga-supervisor default runtime is store-backed (assert engine has a store / state persists).
regression: existing packages/plugin-sagas-core/tests/runtime/saga-store_test.ts and saga-engine tests still pass (engine store-aware path unchanged).
planGateSelfCheck: Research present and current: PASS — every cited file:line re-verified on current main; one unverifiable docs-site sub-evidence explicitly dropped; read-path divergence verified. Decisions locked: PASS — 9 locked decisions with rationale (KV-store-in-plugin mirroring triggers, no Prisma write path, warn-seam in core). Open-decision sweep: PASS — the only open decision (read-path reconciliation) is listed and marked deferred-safe with justification that it forces no rework. Commit slices: PASS — 8 ordered slices, each names proof + gate + files. Risk register: PASS — R1-R4 with mitigations. Gate set: PASS — ARCHETYPE-3/5 F-gates named, scoped check/test/lint/fmt wrappers, e2e:cli correctly excluded (no scaffold change). Deferred scope explicit: PASS. jsr-audit: PASS — applied to planned surface, slow-type/Deno-re-export risks named and cleared. Residual FAIL_PLAN risk: LOW — only plausible challenge is whether the durable default belongs in core vs plugin; plan locks plugin with the triggers precedent as justification, pre-empting that objection.
openQuestions: DEFERRED-SAFE: should the HTTP read path (v1-handlers Prisma `db.sagaInstance` branch + getSagaDb kvdex branch) be reconciled to read from KvSagaStore? Locked as a SEPARATE slice — it forces no rework of the store contract or engine wiring delivered here, so deferring is safe.

MINOR: whether to keep the legacy `sagas.prisma` SagaInstance/SagaExecutionHistory tables as a documented read-model projection (chosen) or remove them. Locked: keep as projection, comment-only correction, to avoid migration churn this slice.
risks: KV optimistic-write semantics must match MemorySagaStore stale-version rejection exactly, or engine concurrency tests diverge — covered by parity tests.
Standalone runner now opens Deno.Kv by default; environments without KV permission could fail at startup — mitigated by env-path config + injection seam + :memory: in tests.
Core warn-on-storeless risks log noise — mitigated by one-time guard and NoopLogger default.

Read-path divergence means API list/get endpoints will NOT immediately reflect engine-written state until the deferred reconciliation slice lands; must be called out in the umbrella PR so it is not mistaken for a regression.
